deep_space_probes.name: Voyager 1
deep_space_probes.milestone_headline: First human-made object to reach interstellar space
deep_space_probes.milestone_detail: Crossed the heliopause on Aug 25, 2012, and is now the most distant human-made object from Earth.
data_products: plasma wave measurements from interstellar space
deep_space_probes.description: Voyager 1's cameras were switched off in 1990 after its 'Pale Blue Dot' photo, and NASA has been shutting down its remaining science instruments one by one to conserve its dwindling power supply -- the cosmic ray subsystem was turned off in February 2026 and the low-energy charged particle instrument in April 2026. As of mid-2026, only the magnetometer and Plasma Wave Subsystem are still switched on.

deep_space_probes.name: Voyager 2
deep_space_probes.milestone_headline: Only spacecraft to visit all four giant planets
deep_space_probes.milestone_detail: Flew by Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune, then reached interstellar space on Nov 5, 2018.
data_products: plasma wave measurements from interstellar space
deep_space_probes.description: Like its twin, Voyager 2's cameras were switched off in 1990. Its plasma science instrument was switched off in October 2024, and its cosmic ray subsystem is scheduled for shutdown in 2026, as NASA conserves its dwindling power supply. Its magnetometer and Plasma Wave Subsystem are still returning real data from interstellar space.

deep_space_probes.name: Pioneer 10
deep_space_probes.milestone_headline: First spacecraft to cross the asteroid belt and fly by Jupiter
deep_space_probes.milestone_detail: Returned the first close-up images of Jupiter in 1973.
deep_space_probes.description: Contact with Pioneer 10 was lost in January 2003 -- none of its instruments have reported data since. The distance/speed shown here is NASA/JPL's tracking-based estimate of where it physically is, not something the spacecraft itself is transmitting.

deep_space_probes.name: Pioneer 11
deep_space_probes.milestone_headline: First spacecraft to fly by Saturn
deep_space_probes.milestone_detail: Returned the first close-up images of Saturn's rings in 1979.
deep_space_probes.description: Last contact with Pioneer 11 was in 1995 -- none of its instruments have reported data since. The distance/speed shown here is NASA/JPL's tracking-based estimate of where it physically is, not something the spacecraft itself is transmitting.
